items.title: Why Starlink is so important to SpaceX's IPOWhen analysts and investors talk about a potential SpaceX IPO, they are, in large part, talking about Starlink.

Chart: Software stocks are plunging
Shaquille O'Neal on the NBA dunk contest: 'It's been sucky'

The housing market is shifting toward buyers. Just not in New Jersey.
Used car prices hit highest levels since 2023, and could push higher
SpaceX: How to buy in before the IPO
Three Oil Supertankers Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
A Panicked Race for Barrels Is Gripping the Global Oil Market
Aegea Revenue Jumps 21% After Delayed Results Spark Bond Rout
Boeing’s Moon Rocket Faces Uncertain Future Under Trump’s NASA
From coffee to home prices, costs are up everywhere
Japan Bets $16 Billion to Propel Rapidus in Global AI Chip Race
Here’s a List of Gulf Energy Infrastructure Damaged in Iran War

3 ways investors can buy into SpaceX nowSpaceX's (SPAX.PVT) upcoming initial public offering (IPO) may end up being the largest on record.Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian explains how potential SpaceX investors should be thinking about buying in before the IPO.
2026 tax season: Can I write off my rent, new car, or dog?
The many ways consumers are being hit by the Iran war

Insider Sells Shares in Offbeat Oil and Gas Royalty Stock. Should You Too?One of the largest U.S. mineral rights holders, Black Stone Minerals, reported a notable insider sale amid continued royalty-driven growth.
Here's What the Futures Markets Are Saying About Oil and the Conflict in the Persian GulfThe answer might surprise investors looking to manage risk under uncertain market conditions.
Tell us: How much inflation can your budget take?Sharply rising gas prices drove inflation to 3.3% in March, the highest rate in nearly two years.
Fed Chair Jerome Powell's 6-Word Warning to Wall Street Still Holds True More Than 6 Months LaterA rarely offered observation by Jerome Powell underscores one of the stock market's biggest risks.
US tariff revenue dropped by over $4 billion in March. It's down almost 30% since October.Tariff revenue totaling $22.15 billion came into US coffers in March, yet another monthly decline.

From coffee to home prices, costs are up everywhereThough overall inflation is somewhat tame at an annual rate of 2.4% — with average hourly earnings growth so far outpacing that increase — Americans continue to reel from prices that soared during the pandemic, never came back to earth, and keep ticking higher.

Consumer sentiment sinks to record low in April as US-Iran war tanks economic outlookConsumer sentiment in April fell sharply to a record low as high energy prices and market volatility weighed on American households.

Why the Fed is likely to hold on rates despite soaring energy pricesThe first major inflation reading after the Iran war began showed a jump in energy prices that pulled up overall inflation but didn't filter through to core inflation.

CPI inflation soars by most since 2022 as gas prices biteHeadline CPI inflation clocked in 3.3% higher than a year ago while being up 0.9% on a monthly basis, a rapid acceleration from February's levels.

Gap between high-income households' wage growth and everyone else is at its widest since 2015After-tax wage growth is accelerating faster among higher-income households as other households trail behind, a new Bank of America Institute report shows.
